title: polypodium lineare var. schraderi
intro: Polypodium lineare var. schraderi, commonly known as the Schrader's polypody, is a variety of the Polypodium lineare species, a type of fern native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of Schrader's polypody include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and boost the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made by drying the plant material and then steeping it in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
